pattern: Scan the string once, grouping maximal runs of identical letters, and emit the start and end index of every run whose length reaches three.
hints: Identical adjacent letters form a run; you only care about runs that are long enough.
Track where the current run began; when the character changes, you know the run just ended at the previous index.

Walk the string remembering the start index of the current run. At each boundary (the letter differs from the next, or the end is reached), if the run spans at least three positions append [start, end] where end is the last index of the run.

edge_inputs.description: single large group in the middle
several large groups including one at the end
single character cannot form a large group
exactly three identical letters is the smallest large group
a run of two is not large even when adjacent to a large run
entire string is one large group
large group at the very start

stress.description: random strings over a tiny alphabet so long runs occur frequently
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    letters = 'abc'
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
